fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 65 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False, and skips clamping when clamp is True. It should be `if clamp:` to clamp when clamping is requested.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True and x is outside [x0,x1], the branch that performs clamping should be entered; but with `not clamp`, clamping is skipped when clamp=True.
The then-branch of the if-not-clamp check should only execute when clamp is False (i.e., clamping is disabled), but the inverted condition causes it to execute when clamp is False instead of True.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the final y value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the bug causes y__5 to be unclamped when clamp=True.

After computing y__1 via linear interpolation, when clamp=False no clamping should occur so y__1 should equal the raw interpolation result; the bug prevents this by applying clamping in the wrong branch.
